LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ANDERSON, ROWE & BUCKLEY, INC.'S MOTION FOR A DETERMINATION THAT DEFENDANT/CROSS-DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA DRYWALL CO.'S SETTLEMENT IS NOT IN GOOD FAITH UNDER CODE CIV PROC SEC 877.6 IS DENIED. CALIFORNIA DRYWALL CO.'S APPLICATION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT MEETS THE REQUIREMENTS OF CCP 877.6(A)(2). DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANT ANDERSON ROW & BUCKLEY, INC. ("ARB") HAS NOT CARRIED ITS BURDEN OF SHOWING THE SETTLEMENT IS "OUT OF THE BALL PARK" AS TO DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANT'S CALIFORNIA DRYWALL CO.'S PROPORTIONAL LIABILITY. (SEE TECH-BILT V. WOODWARD-CLYDE & ASSOCIATES (1985) 38 CAL.3D 488.) ARB'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-02-2014 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-24-2014 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jun-10-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Ntc Of Mtn For A Determination That Deft/Cross-Deft California Drywall Co.'S Settlement Is Not In Good Faith Under Code Civ Proc Sec 877.6
Off Calendar Dec-24-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEAANCES - ATTY. DANIEL C. TAYLOR OF GILBERT KELLY, TEL. NO. 352-6407 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JOSHUA H. WILLERT OF CHRISTENSEN EHRET, TEL. NO. (916) 443-6909 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT ANDERSON ROWE & BUCKLEY INCORPORATED;
ATTY. DANIEL MCCRARY OF THE LAW OFFICES OF BEVERLY E. NARAYAN, TEL. NO. 227-2323 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT CA DRY WALL.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ANDERSON, ROWE & BUCKLEY, INC.'S Motion For A Determination That Defendant/Cross-Defendant California Drywall Co.'s Settlement Is Not In Good Faith Under Code Civ Proc Sec 877.6 is Denied. California Drywall Co.'s application for determination of good faith settlement meets the requirements of CCP 877.6(a)(2). Defendant/Cross-Complainant Anderson Row & Buckley, Inc. ("ARB") has not carried its burden of showing the settlement is "out of the ball park" as to Defendant/Cross-Complainant's California Drywall Co.'s proportional liability. (See Tech-Bilt v. Woodward-Clyde & Associates (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488.) ARB's request for judicial notice is granted. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
